pattern: Matching with '.' and '*' is a classic two-dimensional DP where each '*' branches into using zero copies or consuming one more character.
hints: Ask whether the first characters match, but treat a following '*' specially because it governs the character before it.
A '*' gives two choices: ignore the starred element entirely, or consume one matching character and stay on the same pattern position.

Let dp[i][j] mean s[i:] matches p[j:]. When p[j+1] is '*', dp[i][j] is true if dp[i][j+2] (zero copies) holds, or the first char matches and dp[i+1][j] holds. Otherwise it needs a single-character match plus dp[i+1][j+1].

edge_inputs.description: star must be allowed to match zero copies
pattern longer than the string via zeroing stars
interleaved stars where greedy matching fails
dot matches a single arbitrary character
star of dot consuming the whole string then needing more

stress.description: short strings and patterns over a tiny alphabet with frequent dots and stars
stress.note: tiny alphabet and frequent stars stress the zero-copy branch; lengths capped at 20
